observedMutationClass: skill description narrowing, global rule fallback wording, trigger matrix boundary clarification, reference artifact ownership correction, triage category label normalization, and recurrence closeout shard update
acceptanceEvidenceClass: description word-budget check, trigger matrix and registry comparison, rules:sync-closeout, and full rules lint; no gameplay or Unity runtime acceptance evidence
rootCause: Several routing descriptions used broad shared trigger tokens instead of the intended owner/lifecycle/fallback boundary, so jg-doc-lifecycle could over-trigger on ordinary plan authoring and jg-unity-workflow could overlap too broadly with generic rule-unity. A reference Firebase Hosting doc also listed runnable/config paths as owned artifacts, and a triage category label looked like a real docs path.
prevention: Narrow repo-local skill descriptions to owner/lifecycle/routing signals, keep rule-unity as a generic fallback/reference, mirror the boundary in ops.skill-routing-registry and ops.skill-trigger-matrix, set the Firebase reference doc to artifacts: none with an explicit non-owner note, and replace the path-like triage label with a non-path category label. This change does not alter existing hard-fail coverage for active plan artifact owner shape drift, active plan concrete artifact owner collision, or progress evidence overload.
verification: Description word-budget check for edited skills; npm run --silent rules:sync-closeout with RULES_LINT_CHANGED_FILES scoped to this rules-only change; npm run --silent rules:lint; manual verification of external global skill path C:/Users/SOL/.codex/skills/rule-unity/SKILL.md
